content: We can leverage the intrinsic reward signals from ICM/RND curiosity-driven reinforcement learning to dynamically prioritize which state subspaces get allocated to fast, reactive learning versus slow, deliberate search in hybrid planning-learning systems. This dynamic allocation eliminates the common inefficiency of hybrid systems that commit fixed computational capacity to all state regions, regardless of how much new information they hold.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
